project.name: DORA Attestation Gatekeeper
project.description:
NCA-operated gatekeeper API for HSM attestation verification at certificate issuance. Implements the 7-step verification flow required by DORA (EU 2022/2554) for preventive cryptographic key compliance in payment infrastructure, with a tamper-evident audit log designed to satisfy the legal evidence requirements of supervisory inspections and administrative or judicial proceedings.
Legal basis (Union law): DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — substantive obligations: Articles 5(2)(b), 6(10), 9(2), 9(3)(b)(c)(d), 9(4)(d), 16, 28(1)(a), 28(6) (5-year retention of ICT third-party records), 30(2)(c). DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — supervisory framework directly supported by this code: Article 32 (oversight forum, joint committee structure); Article 33(3)(a)(c)(g) (the comprehensive ICT-risk and ICT-audit criteria the Lead Overseer assesses; the audit log + signed receipts produce the evidence for that assessment); Article 35(1)(a) (request for all relevant information and documentation — implemented via /v1/audit/witness/{id}, /v1/audit/range, /v1/audit/entity, /v1/audit/export); Article 35(1)(c)(d) (reports of remedial measures, recommendations); Article 35(6)–(11) (periodic penalty payments and procedural guarantees — the audit log is the evidentiary basis); Article 36 (cross-border exercise of supervisory powers); Article 37 (formal request for information procedure); Article 38 (general investigations — examine documents, take copies, summon representatives); Article 39 (on-site inspections — the audit log is the off-site evidence equivalent); Article 40 (ongoing supervision via the Joint Examination Team — the audit log persists across team rotations); Article 41 (harmonisation of supervisory conditions; future RTS under this article will refine the audit-evidence format); Article 42 (competent authorities' follow-up of Lead Overseer recommendations — receipt-publication and key-publication endpoints support this directly); Article 50(2) (administrative powers: access to documents, on-site inspection, corrective measures, telecom-data access); Article 51 (exercise of penalty powers); Article 54 (publication of administrative penalties); Article 55 (professional secrecy — applies to information held in the audit log); Article 56 (data protection; 15-year maximum retention by competent authorities — caps audit-log retention from above where the minimum DORA Art 28(6) of 5 years caps it from below). EBA Regulation (EU) 1093/2010 — Articles 16, 17(4), 17(6), 29, 35(1). GDPR Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — Articles 5(1)(c)(e), 17, 23, 32. NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — Articles 14, 21, 23.
Legal basis (Swedish national law for FI inspection authority): Cybersäkerhetslag (2025:1506) — NIS2 implementation; FI tillsynsorgan för finansiell sektor under 2 kap. och 8 kap. Lag (2004:297) om bank- och finansieringsrörelse — 13 kap. tillsyn Lag (2010:751) om betaltjänster — 8 kap. tillsyn av betaltjänstleverantörer Lag (2007:528) om värdepappersmarknaden — 23 kap. tillsyn Lag (2017:630) om åtgärder mot penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism — 7 kap. tillsyn (kompletterar AML-aspekter av flödet) Förordning (2009:93) med instruktion för Finansinspektionen Förvaltningslag (2017:900) — handläggning vid förvaltningsärenden Offentlighets- och sekretesslag (2009:400) — 30 kap. för finansiella tillsynsärenden; styr vad i auditloggen som kan lämnas ut Bokföringslag (1999:1078) — 7 kap. arkiveringsregler (parallell med DORA Art 28(6) — affärshändelser; minst 7 år)
